mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with Rank IC exceeding 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness triggers wage inflation expectations that compress corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that directly boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate output and earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during expansionary phases with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated policy rates and sub-4.2 percent unemployment creates a restrictive financial environment that suppresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment demand that reduces construction sector revenues and broader economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST changes correlate with positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150 thousand additions confirm robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above 150 thousand fail to precede positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer price inflation and policy rate hikes compress real discount rates and trigger central bank hawkishness, mechanically forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual acceleration condition yields positive forward returns in greater than 65 percent of triggered observations.
